steps.title: Spot the contrast signaled by however
steps.content:
The passage presents two opposing views: political scientists say ads should reduce knowledge gaps (reduce the differences in political knowledge), while cognitive scientists note that prior knowledge helps learning (existing domain knowledge facilitates acquisition).

The word “however” marks this direct contrast.

Choice B states that the ads produced an effect opposite to what political scientists expect, which aligns with the cognitive scientists’ idea that prior knowledge facilitates learning.

The other choices either describe the expected narrowing of gaps, claim the cognitive phenomenon didn’t happen, or restrict the predicted benefit to high‑knowledge voters, none of which follow from the contrast signaled by “however”.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
steps.title: Apply the contrast to the answer choices
